raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "assertive claim linking mental states directly to physical outcomes, functioning as moral/philosophical instruction",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "thoughts exert direct causal control over bodily health and appearance",
    "moral valence of thoughts ('unlawful' vs. 'glad and beautiful') determines physiological results",
    "negative thoughts accelerate aging/decay while positive ones reverse or prevent it"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent as a binary contrast but rests on unsubstantiated mind-body causation; logically coherent within its own premises yet empirically unsupported",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "interpreted metaphorically as encouragement for positive mindset and ethical living, with effects limited to psychological well-being or placebo-level influences",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "taken as literal medical causation, leading individuals to attribute disease solely to 'unlawful thoughts' and neglect evidence-based health factors"
}
